abstract.t: This document specifies the integration of the MLDSA44-Ed25519-SHA512 composite signature scheme into the Secure Shell (SSH) protocol. This scheme combines the post-quantum Module-Lattice-Based Digital Signature Algorithm (ML-DSA) with the traditional Ed25519 signature algorithm to provide security against both quantum and classical adversaries.

section.name: Introduction
section.t: The Secure Shell (SSH) protocol is a protocol for secure remote login and other secure network services over an untrusted network.

The advent of quantum computing poses a threat to traditional asymmetric cryptography. Post-quantum algorithms like ML-DSA have been developed to mitigate this threat. However, during the transition period, it is desirable to use hybrid schemes that combine post-quantum and traditional algorithms to protect against both quantum attacks and potential vulnerabilities in newly standardized post-quantum schemes.
This document specifies a composite signature scheme for SSH, mapping the COMPSIG-MLDSA44-Ed25519-SHA512 scheme defined in to the SSH protocol .

section.name: Requirements Language
section.t: The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "NOT RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in BCP 14 when, and only when, they appear in all capitals, as shown here.

section.name: The ssh-mldsa44-ed25519@openssh.com Algorithm
section.t: The algorithm name for this composite signature scheme is "ssh-mldsa44-ed25519@openssh.com".

section.name: Public Key Format
section.t: The "ssh-mldsa44-ed25519@openssh.com" public key has the following format:
The "composite_public_key" string consists of the raw concatenation of the ML-DSA-44 public key (1312 bytes) and the Ed25519 public key (32 bytes), for a total of 1344 bytes.
The format of the ML-DSA-44 public key is defined in . The format of the Ed25519 public key is defined in .

section.name: Signature Format
section.t: Signatures have the following format:
The "composite_signature" string consists of the raw concatenation of the ML-DSA-44 signature (3309 bytes) and the Ed25519 signature (64 bytes), for a total of 3373 bytes.

section.name: Signature Generation
section.t: To sign a message M, the signer first constructs a message representative M' as follows:
section.sourcecode: M' = Prefix || Label || len(ctx) || ctx || SHA512(M)
section.t: Where:

ul.li: len(ctx): A single byte representing the length of the context string. For SSH, this MUST be 0.
ctx: The context string. For SSH, this MUST be the empty string.
SHA512(M): The SHA-512 hash of the original message M.

section.t: The signer then computes:
The final "composite_signature" is the concatenation of mldsa_sig and ed25519_sig.

section.name: Signature Verification
section.t: To verify a signature, the verifier reconstruct M' using the same process as defined for generation. The signature is valid if and only if both underlying signature verifications succeed:

section.name: Private Key Format
section.t: When adding a key to an agent using the SSH_AGENTC_ADD_IDENTITY or SSH_AGENTC_ADD_ID_CONSTRAINED messages, the key data MUST have the following format:
section.sourcecode: string "ssh-mldsa44-ed25519@openssh.com" string composite_public_key string composite_private_key string comment
section.t: Where "composite_public_key" is as defined in the Public Key Format section, and "composite_private_key" is the raw concatenation of the ML-DSA seed (32 bytes) and the Ed25519 seed (32 bytes), for a total of 64 bytes.

section.name: Security Considerations
section.t: The security of this composite scheme depends on the strength of both component algorithms. An adversary must break both ML-DSA-44 and Ed25519 to forge a signature.
Implementations MUST NOT reuse component key material between composite and non-composite keys, or between multiple composite keys.
The use of a unique Label and the inclusion of the algorithm prefix in M' provide non-separability, preventing component signatures from being removed and used in other contexts.

section.name: IANA Considerations
section.t: IANA is requested to register the algorithm name "ssh-mldsa44-ed25519@openssh.com" in the "Public Key Algorithm Names" registry.

author.organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology

front.title: Composite ML-DSA for use in X.509 Public Key Infrastructure
